flashcards.back: Constructors are special member functions of a class that are used to initialize objects.
Destructors are special member functions of a class that are used to clean up objects.
Constructors are called when an object is created.
Destructors are called when an object is about to be destroyed.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of constructors?
flashcards.back: The purpose of constructors is to initialize the object's data members.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of destructors?
flashcards.back: The purpose of destructors is to release any resources that the object has acquired.
No, constructors cannot have return types.
No, destructors cannot have return types.
A copy constructor is a constructor that creates a copy of an existing object.
A default constructor is a constructor that has no parameters.
A parameterized constructor is a constructor that has parameters.

flashcards.front: Why are constructors and destructors important?
flashcards.back: Constructors and destructors are important because they help manage the lifetime of objects and prevent memory leaks.
